title: Execution and generated-code tools expose explicit safety boundaries
summary: Function execution requires an explicit invocation against saved source, while generated project code is previewed and applied only by a second explicit action; neither workflow claims semantic certainty it cannot derive.
details: Assembly and probes refuse modified source buffers rather than compiling stale on-disk bytes under a visually newer buffer.
A function probe executes user/project code only after DansRunFunction and never expands into automatic background execution.
Code generators distinguish parser-known structure from unknown domain semantics: inferred nested validity checks are generated, scalar predicates remain for the owner to define.
Preview buffers render through Neovim, and apply changes an ordinary modifiable buffer without automatically writing it.
Whole-header creation is allowed only into an empty/new target; non-empty headers require incremental declaration insertion.
rationale: Compiler flags and syntax trees provide structure but cannot prove that a function is side-effect-free, link-complete, hardware-independent, or safe to execute.
Header ownership, public API intent, and scalar validity predicates are design knowledge rather than syntax facts.
Explicit preview/application preserves fast automation without disguising inference as correctness.
alternatives: Automatically save source before compiling; rejected because a tooling command should not commit unrelated editor state.
Run any function after inferring default arguments; rejected because construction and side effects are not reliably inferable.
Write generated headers and validation code immediately; rejected because API placement and domain predicates require review.

provenance.reference: conversation 2026-07-12 requested tooling and debugger deferral
provenance.note: Owner requested aggressive utility automation while explicitly shelving the debugger project.
Boundaries reflect what compile databases and Tree-sitter can and cannot establish.

evidence.claim: Saved-source checks, source integrity, explicit invocation, and preview-before-apply lifecycles are covered.

questions: Why does assembly or the function runner require saving first?
Does generated C++ get written automatically?
Why are scalar validity checks not invented?

last_verified.method: Behavior audit against focused interaction tests.
